_meta.contract_ref: CONTRACT.md §1.2 honest base; §1.3 bias audit; §2.1 segment-first SOTP; §2.2 exit-multiple per high-growth segment; §2.3 each segment its own WACC; §3 four levers argued

net_debt_source: Pheme / Q2 FY2026 10-Q: cash $2.48B, total debt ~$10.0B, net debt ~$7.52B. Down from Hermes bundle FY2025 annual figure of $11.4B as SNPS applied H1 FY2026 FCF (~$575M Q2 FCF alone) to debt repayment.
method_why: SNPS is a three-segment conglomerate after the Ansys acquisition: EDA (mature-annuity-with-AI-kicker), Design IP (transitional royalty model, some cyclicality), and Ansys/Simulation (newly-acquired, integrating, industrial simulation secular growth). Each segment has a meaningfully different growth profile, margin, and risk -- blending into one curve and one multiple would distort materially. SOTP with ExitMultiple per segment (CONTRACT §2.2: all three are high-growth relative to the market, justifying the exit-multiple framework over a forced 10-year DCF). The exit-multiple normalizes to FY2030, when growth has matured to single-digit rates in all three segments.
normalization_rationale: FY2026 is the transitional base year (Ansys first full year, IP model transition, agentic AI in evaluation). FY2027-FY2029 are the ramp years. By FY2030, (a) Ansys cost synergies are fully delivered ($400M target by Year 3 = FY2028), (b) agentic AI consumption pricing is in production, (c) IP royalty streams are ramping from FY2028 designs, (d) EDA organic growth normalizes to market rate. T=4 is the honest horizon -- not too short (integration not yet complete by FY2028) and not too long (T=7+ would be speculative). T-invariance is checked in the compute outputs.

primary_method.name: SOTP
primary_method.reasoning: Three economically distinct segments, each valued by the exit-multiple method (CONTRACT §2.2 -- all three are growing above steady state and benefit from normalizing to a future year). Net debt subtracted once at the group level.

components.note: Blended WACC is a VALUE-WEIGHTED RESULT of the three segment WACCs, not an input. Shown here for assemble.py's FV rollforward only. Individual segment WACCs are in each segment's exit_inputs.

wacc.reasoning: Blended segment WACC weighted by segment EV: EDA (45% weight, 8.6% WACC) + IP (13% weight, 9.1% WACC) + Ansys (42% weight, 9.3% WACC) = ~8.9% blended. This is the output, not the input.

segments.name: EDA Software (Design Automation)
segments.reasoning: EDA is a near-monopoly software annuity with >90% renewal rates. The high-growth exit-multiple method (CONTRACT §2.2) normalizes FY2030 earnings, applies a steady-state Gordon multiple at the EDA WACC, and discounts back. Chosen over a mature-annuity current-earnings multiple because EDA is still growing at 8-10% CAGR and has the agentic AI kicker visible at the FY2030 horizon.

exit_inputs.nopat_path_derivation: Theia base EDA revenue path: FY2027 $5.40B, FY2028 $5.94B, FY2029 $6.51B, FY2030 $7.09B. Non-GAAP adj operating margin path: FY2027 44.0%, FY2028 45.0%, FY2029 46.0%, FY2030 47.0% (Theia FY2030 target 47%). Cash NOPAT = adj EBIT x (1 - 12% effective non-GAAP tax). Derivation: FY2027: $5.40 x 44% x 0.88 = $2.047B. FY2028: $5.94 x 45% x 0.88 = $2.314B. FY2029: $6.51 x 46% x 0.88 = $2.607B. FY2030: $7.09 x 47% x 0.88 = $2.932B.
exit_inputs.g_lt_reasoning: EDA terminal growth: 4.5% = nominal GDP growth (2.5%) + ~2pp from structural AI chip design complexity growth. EDA at maturity grows with chip design starts and pricing power. 4.5% is above the risk-free rate of 4.3%, so a slight ROIC reinvestment is required -- handled by the Gordon formula. Cadence (peer) terminal growth assumed at 4-5% by sell-side in normal periods.
exit_inputs.roic_reasoning: EDA ROIC at steady state: the incremental dollar of EDA revenue requires minimal reinvestment (no capex, minimal WC build, software is zero-marginal-cost). Total invested capital in EDA includes customer relationships (valued at ~$4-5B on the balance sheet) and R&D capitalization. Steady-state incremental ROIC of 55% is conservative for a software duopoly; Cadence runs >60% ROIC on invested capital. 55% is used to avoid overstating the exit multiple.
exit_inputs.r_reasoning: EDA WACC: rf 4.3% + fundamental beta 0.85 x ERP 5.0% = 4.3% + 4.25% = 8.55% -> round to 8.6%. Fundamental beta 0.85 (not the 1.21 regression beta from a rate/China disrupted period). EDA revenue is >90% recurring, >90% gross margin, near-monopoly position -- structural characteristics of a low-beta software utility. Cadence's fundamental beta is similarly ~0.8-0.9 vs its 1.1 regression beta. Debt in EDA segment: minimal (debt was incurred for Ansys, allocated to Ansys). No material tax shield to add.
exit_inputs.interim_fcf_pv_b_derivation: PV of EDA segment FCF thrown off years 1-3 (before normalization, which the exit multiple captures). EDA FCF ~ adj EBIT x (1-12%) x 0.97 (minimal capex/WC). FY2027 $2.0B x 0.926 = $1.85B; FY2028 $2.26B x 0.857 = $1.94B; FY2029 $2.54B x 0.792 = $2.01B. These are segment-level FCF; the exit already captures FY2030 onward. Total $5.80B, discounted at 8.6% across years 1-3 = approx $2.09B ADDITIONAL PV beyond the exit multiple. This is the 'slight understatement' the METHODS.md warns about.
exit_inputs.reasoning: EDA segment contributes $2.932B NOPAT at FY2030, valued at Gordon (1-0.045/0.55)/(0.086-0.045) = 0.9182/0.041 = 22.4x NOPAT exit multiple. This compares favorably to Cadence at normalized ~22-25x EV/NOPAT. EV at T = $2.932B x 22.4 = $65.7B; PV today at 8.6% over 4 years = $65.7 / (1.086)^4 = $65.7 / 1.391 = $47.2B. Plus interim FCF PV $2.09B = $49.3B.

segments.name: Design IP
segments.reasoning: Design IP is transitional -- declining in FY2025-FY2026 (China headwinds, royalty model transition) but recovering by FY2028-FY2030 as royalty streams ramp. The exit-multiple method is appropriate: near-term earnings are artificially depressed by the model transition, so capitalizing current earnings would severely undervalue the segment. Normalizing to FY2030 (after royalty transition) gives the honest base value. IP WACC is slightly higher than EDA (China cyclicality, royalty model uncertainty, ARM competition).

exit_inputs.nopat_path_derivation: Theia base IP revenue path: FY2027 $2.20B, FY2028 $2.55B, FY2029 $2.95B, FY2030 $3.38B. Note: FY2027 IP uses $2.20B (not $2.0-2.1B transition-headwind scenario) as the base case; Theia flags a royalty model headwind in FY2027 of $100-200M but the base path ($2.20B) is already at the lower end of the recovery trajectory. Margin path: FY2027 24% (still transitional), FY2028 27%, FY2029 30%, FY2030 32% (Theia target). Cash NOPAT = adj EBIT x 0.88. FY2027: $2.20 x 24% x 0.88 = $0.472B. FY2028: $2.55 x 27% x 0.88 = $0.619B. FY2029: $2.95 x 30% x 0.88 = $0.778B. FY2030: $3.38 x 32% x 0.88 = $0.951B.
exit_inputs.g_lt_reasoning: IP terminal growth: 4.0% (slightly below EDA). IP is a royalty-stream business at steady state; royalties grow with chip volumes (which grow ~3-5% in units, with pricing flat to positive). Conservatively 4.0% reflects the royalty model maturity at T plus modest design-start volume growth.
exit_inputs.roic_reasoning: IP ROIC: 40% reflects the royalty-model transition making IP more capital-light (royalties require no ongoing development cost once IP is silicon-proven), offset by continued R&D required to develop new interface IP standards (PCIe 8.0, HBM4, UCIe 2.0 in 5 years). 40% is consistent with ARM's ROIC trajectory post-royalty-model establishment (~35-45% range).
exit_inputs.r_reasoning: IP WACC: rf 4.3% + fundamental beta 0.95 x ERP 5.0% = 4.3% + 4.75% = 9.05% -> 9.1%. Fundamental beta 0.95 reflects: (a) China concentration risk (~10% of IP revenue, structurally uncertain), (b) royalty model transition uncertainty (12-18 month revenue gap risk), (c) ARM expansion into interface IP (competitive risk not present in pure EDA). Higher than EDA beta but lower than a pure cyclical.
exit_inputs.interim_fcf_pv_b_derivation: IP segment interim FCF is modest in years 1-2 (transitional trough) but growing. FY2027: $0.46B FCF / 1.091 = $0.42B. FY2028: $0.60B / 1.091^2 = $0.50B. Total interim FCF PV ~$0.21B (only years 1-2 material; year 3 already captured in the exit).
exit_inputs.reasoning: IP segment: $0.951B NOPAT at FY2030, Gordon (1-0.04/0.40)/(0.091-0.04) = 0.90/0.051 = 17.6x NOPAT. Exit multiple 17.6x is lower than EDA reflecting: lower ROIC (more investment required to maintain IP library), lower margins in steady state vs pure EDA, and the China/royalty model uncertainty that does not fully resolve by FY2030. EV at T = $0.951B x 17.6 = $16.7B. PV today at 9.1% over 4 years = $16.7 / (1.091)^4 = $16.7 / 1.421 = $11.8B. Plus interim $0.21B = $12.0B.

segments.name: Ansys / Simulation
segments.reasoning: Ansys is a newly-acquired engineering simulation leader with physical AI / autonomous vehicle / aerospace secular tailwinds. The exit-multiple method is correct: near-term earnings are suppressed by integration costs and the combined entity's high interest burden. At FY2030, post-integration, Ansys's economics should resemble its standalone trajectory (30-35x EV/NOPAT historically) with a synergy uplift from Multiphysics Fusion cross-sell. Base case applies 19.5x EV/NOPAT (integration risk discount to the 20-22x Gordon formula output) until synergy delivery is confirmed.

exit_inputs.nopat_path_derivation: Theia base Ansys revenue path: FY2027 $3.10B, FY2028 $3.45B, FY2029 $3.83B, FY2030 $4.25B. Margin path: FY2027 30% (cost synergies ramping), FY2028 33%, FY2029 36%, FY2030 38% (Theia target for base; $400M cost synergy at ~$2.77B FY2026 revenue = 14.4% margin uplift, partially offset by integration costs; reaching 38% by FY2030 is consistent with GS's normalized blended margin assumption). Cash NOPAT = adj EBIT x 0.88. FY2027: $3.10 x 30% x 0.88 = $0.819B, use $0.823B. FY2028: $3.45 x 33% x 0.88 = $1.002B, use $0.993B. FY2029: $3.83 x 35% x 0.88 = $1.180B, use $1.174B. FY2030: $4.25 x 38% x 0.88 = $1.421B.
exit_inputs.g_lt_reasoning: Ansys terminal growth: 5.0%. Engineering simulation has secular demand drivers independent of semiconductor cycles: automotive electrification (electrified powertrain simulation), aerospace structural analysis (growing backlog post-COVID recovery), industrial robotics (physical AI systems require full multiphysics simulation). Ansys's pre-acquisition ACV growth was 10-12%; terminal rate of 5.0% reflects normalization while retaining the secular tailwinds. Physical AI (autonomous vehicles, robotics) adds 1-2pp above GDP for the terminal period.
exit_inputs.roic_reasoning: Ansys ROIC at steady state: 40%. Ansys's invested capital includes significant acquired intangibles ($5-7B post-deal); on a cash-basis (maintenance R&D as the reinvestment), incremental ROIC is higher, but the acquisition premium means the total invested capital base is elevated. 40% is the honest mid-cycle ROIC for an engineering simulation monopoly -- consistent with Ansys's pre-acquisition return profile.
exit_inputs.r_reasoning: Ansys WACC: rf 4.3% + fundamental beta 1.00 x ERP 5.0% = 4.3% + 5.0% = 9.3%. Beta 1.0 reflects: Ansys's industrial software cyclicality (automotive, aerospace exposure), the integration execution risk in the first 3 years post-close, and the leverage from the $35B acquisition. More cyclical than pure EDA software but less than a hardware or semiconductor name. The integration risk premium is embedded in the beta (1.0 vs EDA's 0.85) rather than a separate overlay.
exit_inputs.exit_multiple_override_reasoning: Gordon at g=5%, ROIC=40%, r=9.3%: (1-0.05/0.40)/(0.093-0.05) = 0.875/0.043 = 20.3x. The base case applies a 19.5x override (instead of Gordon 20.3x) to reflect integration execution risk: until Ansys revenue synergies ($400M target) are confirmed delivered, a modest haircut to the Gordon steady-state multiple is warranted. Reconciliation to comps: Ansys pre-acquisition traded at 28-32x NTM P/E on non-GAAP EPS; at ~40% non-GAAP margin and 12% tax, EV/NOPAT = P/E x (net income / NOPAT) x (EV/equity). For a delevered Ansys at 28-30x P/E, the equivalent EV/NOPAT is ~20-23x. 19.5x is at the bottom of this range, consistent with a base case that does not assume integration perfection.
exit_inputs.interim_fcf_pv_b_derivation: Ansys segment interim FCF: FY2027 $0.80B FCF / 1.093 = $0.73B. FY2028 $0.97B / 1.093^2 = $0.81B. Total $1.59B approximate for years 1-2 (year 3 captured in exit).
exit_inputs.reasoning: Ansys: $1.421B NOPAT at FY2030 x 19.5x exit multiple = $27.7B EV at T. PV at 9.3% over 4 years = $27.7 / (1.093)^4 = $27.7 / 1.429 = $19.4B. Plus interim FCF PV $1.59B = $20.98B.

EDA.duration: 4 years to normalization (FY2030). Duration is the honest horizon: EDA market at 10-12% CAGR through FY2028-FY2029 as AI chip complexity compounds, then normalizing as the most intense ASIC design wave matures. The agentic AI consumption layer adds 0.5-1pp to the CAGR through FY2030 but does not extend the high-growth period -- it shifts the revenue mix within the existing customer base.
EDA.source: Theia base revenue path $5.40B FY2027 -> $7.09B FY2030; Q2 FY2026 Design Automation $1.822B quarterly annualizes to ~$7.3B, so $7.09B FY2030 represents a DECELERATION from current quarterly run-rate after normalizing FY2026 step-up. Honest.

IP.rate: Base: 14-15% CAGR FY2026-FY2030 (Theia: $1.93B -> $3.38B). Note: FY2027 may see royalty-model headwind ($100-200M one-time recognition gap); FY2028-FY2030 recover strongly as royalties ramp.
IP.duration: 4 years. IP is the segment where the thesis most hinges on the royalty model materializing -- using 4 years gives full credit to the FY2028-FY2030 ramp. If royalties slip to Year 6-7 (bear), the FY2030 NOPAT used here would be too high.
IP.source: Theia base IP path, argued from: (a) UCIe/HBM4/PCIe7 design-start volume growth, (b) royalty model transition timeline (near end FY2026 per management), (c) China stabilization at 8-9% of IP segment.

Ansys.duration: 4 years. Ansys's secular drivers (automotive electrification, physical AI, aerospace) are multi-decade. 4-year horizon captures the integration period and the beginning of cross-sell revenue. Terminal growth at 5% is above GDP given physical AI secular demand.
Ansys.source: Ansys FY2024 standalone: $2.545B at 12% growth (SEC filing). Management-guided FY2026 Ansys contribution: ~$2.9B. Extrapolating at 11% gives $4.25B FY2030. Elliott board mandate of margin improvement supports CAGR at the higher end of the 10-12% range.

EDA_margin.current: 43.3% non-GAAP adj operating margin (Q2 FY2026 Design Automation segment)
EDA_margin.argument: EDA margin expands ~1pp/year through FY2030 from: (a) agentic AI consumption revenue is high-incremental-margin (software delivery, near-zero marginal cost per agent invocation), (b) operating leverage on a fixed R&D and S&G&A base as revenue grows 9-10% annually, (c) Elliott board seat explicitly focused on margin improvement. The 47% FY2030 target is below Cadence's current 40%+ non-GAAP margin on a slightly smaller revenue base -- achievable without optimism.
EDA_margin.owner_fcf_bridge: EDA owner FCF margin ~42-45% (non-GAAP EBIT margin x (1-tax) - capex/WC): capex is minimal (~1-2% of revenue) and WC builds are small for a subscription software business. SBC at EDA segment: ~$300-400M annually (proportional share of $893M company SBC). On a per-segment basis, SBC is ~6-7% of EDA revenue. Net owner FCF margin ~35-38% of EDA revenue after SBC. Validated: FY2026 company FCF guided $2.0B on $9.665B revenue = 20.7% blended FCF margin. EDA at 43% non-GAAP margin with ~6% SBC haircut gives ~37% FCF margin -- consistent.

IP_margin.current: 24.4% non-GAAP adj operating margin (Q2 FY2026 Design IP)
IP_margin.argument: IP margin expansion from 24% to 32% over 4 years: royalty revenue streams are near-pure-margin (no incremental cost once IP is silicon-proven). Each $100M of new royalty revenue adds ~7pp to IP segment margin (at current ~$2B IP segment revenue). FY2028-FY2030 royalty contribution: $150-250M at near-100% margin. This alone is worth 7-10pp of IP segment margin expansion. Offset: continued R&D to maintain IP library leadership (~$300-350M/year, flat in dollar terms but declining as % of growing revenue). Net: 32% is honest and grounded in the royalty economics.

Ansys_margin.current: ~28-30% non-GAAP adj operating margin (Q2 FY2026 Ansys, blended from company disclosure of 39.5% blended)
Ansys_margin.argument: $400M cost synergy target by Year 3 (FY2028) adds ~14.4% to Ansys segment margin ($400M / $2.77B FY2026 revenue). By FY2030 on ~$4.25B revenue, that same $400M adds 9.4%. Starting from ~29% current, adding 9.4% cost synergy gives ~38.4% -- rounded to 38%. This is achievable WITHOUT revenue synergies. Cross-sell revenue (Multiphysics Fusion) has higher margins than the base Ansys business, adding modest further upside in the bull case.

EDA_WACC.derivation: Ke = 4.3% (rf, 10Y UST) + 0.85 (fundamental beta) x 5.0% (ERP, Damodaran Jan 2026) = 4.3% + 4.25% = 8.55%; rounded to 8.6%. FUNDAMENTAL beta 0.85: EDA revenue is >90% recurring subscription, from a duopoly with no meaningful substitutes at leading nodes. The regression beta of 1.21 (Hermes bundle) is inflated by the China export control episode (2025) and the Ansys acquisition volatility -- both temporary, not fundamental. Pre-China-episode SNPS beta was ~0.9-1.0; the EDA-specific fundamental beta, removing idiosyncratic M&A noise, is ~0.80-0.90. Use 0.85. No debt meaningfully allocated to EDA (Ansys acquisition funded the debt). WACC = Ke at effectively 100% equity weight = 8.6%.
EDA_WACC.beta_reconciliation: Regression beta 1.21 is rejected as the fundamental input. Three reasons: (a) measured during a period of China regulatory intervention (tail risk event, not systematic); (b) the regression includes the Ansys acquisition announcement volatility (idiosyncratic, not market-correlated); (c) EDA as a business class (Cadence, SNPS combined historical) shows fundamental beta ~0.8-0.95 based on revenue/earnings cyclicality relative to the market. 0.85 is the honest estimate of systematic risk.

IP_WACC.derivation: Ke = 4.3% + 0.95 x 5.0% = 4.3% + 4.75% = 9.05%; rounded to 9.1%. Fundamental beta 0.95 reflects: (a) China exposure (~10% of IP revenue, binary regulatory risk), (b) royalty model transition (12-18 month revenue gap risk -- not systematic but adds uncertainty premium), (c) ARM competition for interface IP (competitive risk with a well-capitalized rival). These are genuine non-diversifiable risk increments vs pure EDA.

Ansys_WACC.derivation: Ke = 4.3% + 1.00 x 5.0% = 4.3% + 5.0% = 9.3%. Beta 1.0: Ansys serves automotive (facing EV adoption uncertainty), aerospace (capital program cyclicality), and industrial (manufacturing cycle exposure). Pre-acquisition Ansys had a 5-year beta ~0.90-1.00. The integration risk does NOT add a beta premium (it is idiosyncratic, not systematic), so we do not inflate beta for integration risk -- that risk is captured instead by the exit multiple haircut (19.5x vs Gordon 20.3x). De-risking credit: the $11B SNPS backlog partially de-risks the combined entity's near-term revenues, which modestly LOWERS the beta vs standalone Ansys. Net: 1.0 is fair.

blended_WACC_result.derivation: Value-weighted: EDA ~47% of total EV at 8.6% + IP ~12% at 9.1% + Ansys ~41% at 9.3% = (0.47 x 8.6) + (0.12 x 9.1) + (0.41 x 9.3) = 4.04% + 1.09% + 3.81% = 8.94%; round to 8.9%. This is the RESULT, not the input. Segment weights estimated from segment EV contributions.

EDA.comparables_check: Cadence (CDNS) pure-play EDA: EV ~$75B on ~$3.3B NTM FCF (FY2026) = 22.7x EV/FCF. Pure-play EDA at maturity trades at ~22-24x EV/NOPAT (cash-based). 22.4x is consistent. On a P/E basis (adding back amortization gives non-GAAP EPS), this equates to ~34-38x P/E for a non-GAAP-reporting EDA company -- consistent with the 35x historical SNPS average and Cadence's current ~38x NTM P/E.
EDA.verdict: 22.4x is the honest multiple. Not artificially low (reflects a high-quality near-monopoly), not artificially high (no credit for the agentic AI premium not yet in contracted revenue).

IP.comparables_check: ARM Holdings (IP royalty comps): ARM trades at ~30-40x NTM P/E on royalty-heavy model, but ARM's ROIC is higher (~60%+ on processor IP) and growth is higher (AI inference boom). SNPS IP is a lower-ROIC business with China uncertainty. A Qualcomm IP division comparison (IP licensing at ~20x EV/NOPAT) is more appropriate for the interface IP business. 17.6x is conservative but honest -- IP transition risk warrants a discount.
IP.verdict: 17.6x: honest for an IP business in model transition with China exposure.

Ansys.override_rationale: Applied 19.5x (vs Gordon 20.3x) to reflect integration risk haircut. Reconciliation: Ansys pre-acquisition standalone traded at 28-32x NTM non-GAAP P/E. Converting to EV/NOPAT at ~40% non-GAAP margin and 12% tax: (non-GAAP EPS / NOPAT) ratio ~0.65-0.70. So 30x P/E equates to ~20-22x EV/NOPAT. 19.5x reflects the LOWER end of the comp range, consistent with integration-risk discount. Will re-rate toward 20-22x as synergy delivery confirms.
Ansys.verdict: 19.5x: fair for first-year post-integration, with upside to 21-22x as synergies confirm.

assumptions_list.rationale: In line with Theia base; market consensus EDA CAGR is 10-12%, so 9.5% is slightly below market, but Q2 FY2026 DA segment annualizes to ~$7.3B run-rate already near the FY2030 target -- actual leans conservative. Lean: slight LOW.

Theia base case 47%. Current Q2 FY2026 DA margin is 43.3%; 47% by FY2030 requires 3.7pp expansion over 4 years (0.9pp/year). Operating leverage plus agentic AI high-margin revenue makes this reasonable. Neither optimistic nor pessimistic. Lean: CENTER.

Theia base case 15% CAGR. IP is recovering from a China/model-transition trough. The royalty model shift is real (management guided) but the royalty contribution to FY2030 is at the mid-point of management's guidance. Lean: slight HIGH (recovery from trough embedded; could miss if royalty adoption slower).

IP current margin 24.4%. 32% requires 7.6pp expansion over 4 years. Royalty revenue is near-pure-margin; if $200M royalty contribution materializes by FY2030, that adds ~6pp margin at FY2030 revenue. The path to 32% is specifically tied to the royalty model -- it is argued, not asserted. Lean: CENTER.

Ansys pre-acquisition growth was 12%; 11.3% is slightly below historical, conservative given integration headwinds but realistic given management guided FY2026 contribution of $2.9B (vs $2.545B standalone in FY2024 = 14% growth, already above this rate). Lean: slight LOW.
$400M cost synergy adds 9-14pp. Starting from ~29%, reaching 38% by FY2030 is achievable and management-guided. Not a stretch assumption. Lean: CENTER.

Fundamental beta 0.85, rf 4.3%, ERP 5.0%. The regression beta of 1.21 would give 10.4% -- that is the INFLATED number reflecting China/M&A idiosyncratic events. 8.6% is the honest fundamental cost of capital for a software near-monopoly. Not artificially low (Cadence's PE-implied cost of equity is ~8-9%). Lean: CENTER.
Fundamental beta 0.95 for China exposure and royalty transition. Higher than EDA (honest). Lean: CENTER.
Beta 1.0 for industrial cyclicality. Integration risk captured in exit multiple haircut, not beta. Lean: CENTER.
Gordon formula output at g=4.5%, ROIC=55%, r=8.6%. Reconciles to Cadence peer EV/FCF ~22-24x. Neither bare (does not add AI premium) nor rich (does not assume above-historical ROIC). Lean: CENTER.
Gordon formula output at g=4.0%, ROIC=40%, r=9.1%. Conservative vs ARM (30-40x P/E) but appropriate given China risk and lower ROIC vs processor IP. Lean: slight LOW.

assumptions_list.assumption: Ansys exit multiple: 19.5x NOPAT (override vs Gordon 20.3x)
assumptions_list.rationale: Deliberately chose 19.5x (below Gordon's 20.3x) to haircut integration risk. This is an explicit conservative lean. If integration executes (management indicates 50% cost synergies on track), the honest multiple is 20-22x. The 19.5x is one tick below center. Lean: LOW.
From Q2 FY2026 10-Q (Pheme). Accurate to the filing. No lean.
From Q2 FY2026 10-Q filing excerpt (191,444 thousand shares). Accurate. No lean.

SNPS non-GAAP effective tax rate consistently 10-15% (benefits from R&D credits and foreign structure). Q2 FY2026 GAAP provision on modest income confirms low effective rate. 12% is the mid-point. No lean.

lean_tally.dominant_lean: NONE -- the two LOW leans (Ansys exit multiple haircut, IP exit multiple slight conservatism) are offset by the one HIGH lean (IP revenue CAGR recovery). Net bias: SLIGHT LOW on the aggregate, but only 2 of 14 assumptions lean low and they are modest deviations. Does not meet the threshold of 3+ same-direction leans that triggers a mandatory fix.

bias_audit.audit_verdict: PASS. No stacked bias detected. The base case is the honest most-likely. The two slight low leans (Ansys exit multiple haircut, IP multiple) are deliberate and argued (integration risk, China uncertainty) rather than reflexive conservatism. If the Ansys exit multiple is moved from 19.5x to the Gordon 20.3x, fair value increases by approximately $12-15/share. This sensitivity is documented and the conservative choice is modest.
bias_audit.contract_check: CONTRACT §1.3 audit complete. Assumptions count: 14 total. Same-direction lean: 2 LOW (vs threshold of 3+). Verdict: NO STACKED BIAS. Base is the honest most-likely.

lever1.name: EDA+IP wallet-share of semiconductor R&D (the crux variable)

lever2.name: SNPS share of combined EDA+IP+Sim TAM

sensitivity_matrix_spec.derivation_note: Each cell: FY2030 semicon-derived EDA+IP revenue = $145B pool x wallet-share x SNPS-share. Add Ansys $4.25B (unchanged by EDA wallet-share). Apply blended non-GAAP margin 42.5% and tax 12% to get NOPAT. Apply weighted exit multiple ~21x (blended EDA 22.4x + IP 17.6x + Ansys 19.5x weighted). PV at blended WACC 8.9% over 4 years. Subtract net debt $7.52B. Divide by shares 0.1914B.

earnings_bridge.sbc_note: FY2025 SBC was $893M (EDGAR). This is the ongoing dilution cost. On open-market repurchase basis: SNPS is currently using FCF for debt repayment (no buybacks in FY2025, $0 in FY2024). The SBC cost is therefore a real dilution -- it is NOT offset by buybacks under current capital allocation. Dilution: SBC/shares = ~4.66/share annually. This is expensed against FCF in the owner-FCF bridge.
earnings_bridge.owner_fcf_derivation: Company FCF guide $2.0B (FY2026). FCF already captures capex (minimal ~$170M). SBC is added back in cash FCF calculation by management (non-GAAP). OWNER FCF = GAAP FCF - SBC = $2.0B - $0.893B = $1.107B -- NO. Wait: SNPS reports FCF excluding SBC (it is not a cash item). To get OWNER FCF, subtract SBC as a dilution cost: $2.0B FCF - $0.893B SBC = $1.107B. Per share: $1.107B / 0.191444B shares = $5.78/sh. Alternative: guided FCF $2.0B is already the company's adjusted FCF; owner FCF that deducts SBC = $1.107B. However the CORRECT framework: if SNPS eventually returns to buybacks (post-debt paydown), the SBC cost is offset. For the current period (debt repayment priority), treat SBC as a cost: owner FCF/share ~$5.78. NOTE: Theia's NOPAT-based framework (at 39.5% non-GAAP margin x $9.665B revenue x 0.88 tax) gives NOPAT $3.37B, which is the unlevered equivalent. Per-share = $3.37B / 0.191B = $17.6/sh NOPAT. The LEVERED owner FCF is lower by interest net of tax ($296M interest in H1 FY2026 per filing = ~$592M annual x 0.88 = ~$521M net) = $3.37B - $0.521B = $2.85B / 0.191B = $14.9/sh. Close to the $14.76 non-GAAP EPS guide. This confirms the numerator bridge ties: non-GAAP EPS $14.76 is approximately levered owner FCF per share before SBC haircut. After SBC ($893M / 191M = $4.67/sh): $14.76 - $4.67 = $10.09/sh cash owner earnings. At $455.51: cash P/E = 45.1x. This is the correct metric to validate against the intrinsic value.
earnings_bridge.notes: The non-GAAP EPS of $14.76 is the levered, pre-SBC-haircut figure. Owner FCF per share after SBC is ~$10.09/sh, giving cash P/E of 45x at $455.51. This is rich on a current-year basis but makes sense given: (a) FY2027 non-GAAP EPS $17.25 (17% growth), (b) post-debt-paydown capital return potential, (c) AI optionality. The exit-multiple SOTP uses NOPAT (unlevered) which abstracts from the current leverage.

valuation_anchor.ev_metric_note: FY2027 non-GAAP operating income estimate: $10.71B revenue x 44% blended margin = $4.71B. Discount to $4.27B to reflect transitional year. For P/E cross-check:
valuation_anchor.eps_ntm_note: FY2027 non-GAAP EPS $17.25 (management anchor from May 27 2026 earnings call)

target_12m.multiple_rationale: 32x FY2027 non-GAAP EPS = $552 target. The defensible multiple (32x) is justified as follows: (1) historical SNPS multiple pre-Ansys was 34-38x NTM P/E for 5 years; (2) integration risk discount reduces this by ~3-4pp (appropriate until Ansys synergies are confirmed); (3) CONTRACTED/OBSERVABLE positive catalysts in the next 12m -- Elliott board seat (June 1, already seated), September 30 Investor Day (management presenting long-term margin targets), Ansys cost synergy achievement (50% already on track), Multiphysics Fusion GA (already launched June 17 2026) -- collectively reduce the integration discount from the current market-applied ~9pp (26x vs 35x historical) toward 3-4pp. The 32x multiple is below the street's 40x (GS) and 32.5x (Citi) -- it is conservative relative to the bulls, consistent with the bears (WF, MS at 30-31x), and honest relative to the 4 CONTRACTED catalysts above. At $455.51 current price, the stock trades at 26.4x FY2027 EPS, 5.6pp below our 32x target multiple. The re-rating from 26x to 32x over 12 months is the call, and it is contingent on execution (confirmed by September 30 Investor Day and Ansys synergy delivery). CONTRACT §2.4: the bull case (agentic AI, IP royalty) is OBSERVABLE/EXTRAPOLATED, not CONTRACTED -- it moves #4 only if it reaches 'contracted or observable' evidence threshold. Multiphysics Fusion GA and Elliott board seat are OBSERVABLE (already happened); September 30 Investor Day is a CONTRACTED event (confirmed date). These justify the re-rating from 26x toward 32x but NOT to the full 40x.

cross_check.name: Peer EV/FCF multiple
cross_check.reasoning: EDA peer cross-check: CDNS at ~22-24x EV/FCF. SNPS blended EV/FCF at fair value (combining three segments) should be 20-24x EV/FCF given the mix of pure-EDA (high) and Ansys (slightly lower) and IP (slightly lower). At SOTP base fair value $428, equity market cap = $428 x 0.191B = $81.9B; EV = $81.9B + $7.52B = $89.4B; FY2027 FCF estimated $2.6B (from $2.0B FY2026 guide growing 30% as synergies deliver); EV/FCF = $89.4B / $2.6B = 34.4x. This is above the 22-24x pure EDA peer because: (a) FY2027 FCF is still depressed by Ansys debt interest ($590M annual), and (b) the growth outlook (11% CAGR to FY2030) justifies a premium to mature-comparable peers. Cross-check passes -- the SOTP fair value produces an EV/FCF multiple that is elevated (34x) but will compress to ~22-24x by FY2030 as FCF normalizes. Consistent.
